questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the right order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. Feared by most, loved by some and hunted by many, sharks are one of the most mysterious groups of creatures roaming the Earth today. B. Defined as a fish with a full cartilaginous skeleton and a sleek, streamlined body, a shark can range in size from the two foot pygmy shark, to the colossal 50-foot whale shark. C. There are more than 250 different species of sharks currently identified, making it one of the most diverse animal genera on the planet. D. Sharks are found in every major body of saltwater in the world, but are more common in warmer waters.
questions.explanation: the first sentence should be introductory or a general statement. Here, A is the introductory statement. So, option d is correct.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the highlighted part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed select ‘No improvement required’. There has been a lot of furore ever since the disclosure by them who maintain that it was an unauthorized visit.
options: ever since the disclosure from them
ever for the disclosure by those
ever since the disclosure by those
questions.explanation: Demonstrative pronoun those should be used instead of them.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. below the belt
options: to agree someone by beating him up
questions.explanation: below the belt – to strike unfairly

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: correct spelling is depressed.

questions.question: The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them may contain an error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. If there is no error, select ‘No error’. They have lived / in this apartment / since 2013.
questions.explanation: “They have been living” in place of “They have lived”. Perfect continuous tense shows that some action started sometime in the past and is still continuing. And the format is: Sub + have/has +been + verb’s ing form + object + since/for + point/period of time.

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the right order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. An old mouse, who was wise enough to keep his distance, whispered to a friend, “Many a bag have I seen in my day, but never one with a cat's head”. B. She hoped that the mice would no longer be afraid to come near her. C. A cat, grown feeble with age and no longer able to hunt mice as she once did, thought to herself how she might entice them within reach of her paw. D. Thinking that she might pass herself off for a bag or for a dead cat at least, she suspended herself by the hind legs from a peg.
questions.explanation: the first sentence should be C as it is introducing the situation that the cat is old and weak and is trying to find ways to bring her prey into her reach. The next should be D because it tells about the plan she is thinking of. B is the consequence cat is thinking of and that should be followed by the actual happening. So, the correct sequence is CDBA.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the highlighted segment in the given sentence. If no substitution is required, select ‘No substitution’. The furnitures is ready for the delivery.
questions.explanation: Furniture is an uncountable noun which means its plural cannot be made. So, c is the correct option.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. Please do your task properly.
options: You are requested to do your task properly.
Can you please do your task properly?
You are instructed to do your task properly.
Will you please do your task properly?
questions.explanation: There is no reason for putting question mark in this because there is no question asked. So, option b and d are wrong. Word “please” means it is a request and not an instruction. Therefore, option a is the correct one.Rule: Imperative sentence You are ordered/requested/forbidden, etc + to + v1+ object.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Augment
questions.explanation: Augment – to increaseExtend, spread and expand also mean the same. Curtail – to make something shorter

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. All the patches of the ______ garden have a rich cover of grass now.
questions.explanation: slouching – standing or walking in a lazy way.Stroking – to move something somewhere with a smooth movement.Sprawling – spread over a wide area.Stretching – to pull something to make it larger.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. An olive branch
questions.explanation: An olive branch – something showing intentions for peace

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in reported speech. "I'll see you later," he said.
options: he said that he would see me later.
he said that he would be seeing me later.
he said that he will see you later.
he said that he will be able to see you later.
questions.explanation: As per the rules, future simple tense changes into present conditional tense.Sub + will + v1 + object changes to sub + would + v1 + object.So, “he said that he would be see me later” will be the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. share prices _____ from day to day.
questions.explanation: rotate – spin on axis.Revolves – move around a centreSwing – to and fro motionFluctuate – change frequently from one to another

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: accommodate is the correct spelling.

questions.question: The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them contains an error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. The construction of / the new factory / is led to / a sudden rise of population in our area.
options: a sudden rise of population in our area
questions.explanation: “has led to” in place of “is led to” because the sentence is in the present perfect tense. The present perfect tense links past to the present in some way.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. having more than one possible meaning
questions.explanation: ambiguous – not clear, confusing or having more than one meaningMultilingual – written or printed in several languagesChivalry – men’s polite and respectful behaviour towards womenTerse – concise and rude.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Waver
questions.explanation: Glimmer – a dim and flickering lightResolute – determinedFlicker – light or shine going on and off

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Knave
questions.explanation: truthful and candid mean the same i.e, an honest man.Humble – polite and down to earth.Knave and Rogue both means an unscrupulous man

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Pensive
questions.explanation: Pensive – thinking deeply

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. Treatment of something sacred with disrespect
questions.explanation: Sanctification – to set apart for religious use.Détente – an improvement in relationship between two hostile countries.Veneration – respect towards someone because of his/her charismatic personality

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each blank. The word “archaeology” comes from the Greek word “arkhaios,” which means “ancient.” (1)______ some archaeologists study living cultures, most archaeologists concern (2)______ with the distant past. People have dug (3)______ monuments and collected artefacts for thousands of years. (4)______, these people were not scholars, but looters and grave robbers looking to make money or (5)______ up their personal collections. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 1.
questions.explanation: Although, some archaeologists study living cultures, most archaeologists concern themselves with the distant pastAlthough is used to introduce a subordinate clause which makes the main clause unexpected or surprising because it is contrary to the former.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each blank. The word “archaeology” comes from the Greek word “arkhaios,” which means “ancient.” (1)______ some archaeologists study living cultures, most archaeologists concern (2)______ with the distant past. People have dug (3)______ monuments and collected artefacts for thousands of years. (4)______, these people were not scholars, but looters and grave robbers looking to make money or (5)______ up their personal collections. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 2.
questions.explanation: Although, some archaeologists study living cultures, most archaeologists concern themselves with the distant past.Themselves is the correct pronoun for “archaeologists”.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each blank. The word “archaeology” comes from the Greek word “arkhaios,” which means “ancient.” (1)______ some archaeologists study living cultures, most archaeologists concern (2)______ with the distant past. People have dug (3)______ monuments and collected artefacts for thousands of years. (4)______, these people were not scholars, but looters and grave robbers looking to make money or (5)______ up their personal collections. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 3.
questions.explanation: dug up – find something by digging in the ground.e.g. I dug up an old box in the garden.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each blank. The word “archaeology” comes from the Greek word “arkhaios,” which means “ancient.” (1)______ some archaeologists study living cultures, most archaeologists concern (2)______ with the distant past. People have dug (3)______ monuments and collected artefacts for thousands of years. (4)______, these people were not scholars, but looters and grave robbers looking to make money or (5)______ up their personal collections. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 4.
questions.explanation: often is the most appropriate option out of the given.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each blank. The word “archaeology” comes from the Greek word “arkhaios,” which means “ancient.” (1)______ some archaeologists study living cultures, most archaeologists concern (2)______ with the distant past. People have dug (3)______ monuments and collected artefacts for thousands of years. (4)______, these people were not scholars, but looters and grave robbers looking to make money or (5)______ up their personal collections. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 5.
questions.explanation: build up – an increase of something over a period
